symbols.doc: The failure context handed to an {@link AgentRunner}, plus a ready prompt.

members.name: target
members.doc: The name of the failed target.

members.name: attempt
members.doc: The 1-based recovery attempt.

members.name: command
members.doc: The command line that failed, if known.

members.name: output
members.doc: The captured error output (stderr, or the error message).

members.name: conventions
members.doc: Project conventions (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md), if found.

members.name: prompt
members.doc: A ready-to-use prompt assembled from the fields above.

symbols.name: agentFixer
symbols.doc:
Construct an {@link AgentFixer} from an {@link AgentRunner} and apply the
configuration lambda. Plug the result into a target with `.recoverWith(...)`.
symbols.signature: function agentFixer(run: AgentRunner, configure?: Configure<AgentFixer>): AgentFixer

symbols.name: AgentFixer
symbols.doc:
A fluent agent fixer. Construct one via {@link agentFixer} with a runner, and
attach it to a target with `.recoverWith(...)`. Diagnose/report defaults are
on; file changes happen through the agent, gated to local runs unless
`.allowCI()`.

members.name: allowCI
members.doc: Permit the agent to run (and edit files) on CI; off by default.

members.name: suggest
members.doc:
Propose the agent's changes as **committable inline `suggestion`s** on the
pull request (from its `git diff`) instead of committing them. The build
stays failed — the human applies the suggestions to fix it. Mutually
exclusive with {@link commitFixes} (suggest takes precedence). GitHub only;
elsewhere it falls back to the overview comment.

members.name: commitFixes
members.doc:
After the agent runs, stage all its changes, commit, and push to the current
branch so a healed PR carries the fix. Requires a checkout that can push; a
failed push is reported, not fatal.

members.name: commitMessage
members.doc: Override the commit subject used by {@link commitFixes}.

members.name: noPush
members.doc: Commit the fix but do not push it.

members.name: comment
members.doc: Also post what the agent did as a PR comment (on by default).

members.name: noComment
members.doc: Do not post a PR comment (the job summary is still written).

members.name: commentToken
members.doc: The token used to post the PR comment (defaults to the host's env var).

members.name: criteria
members.doc: Project-specific notes appended to the agent's prompt.

members.name: conventions
members.doc:
Supply the project conventions text directly instead of reading
`CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`. Pass an empty string to send none.

members.name: quiet
members.doc: Suppress the console printout (the summary/comment are still written).

members.name: env
members.doc: The environment reader used to detect CI and the comment host (test seam).

members.name: exec
members.doc: The `git` runner used for committing (test seam).

members.name: readFile
members.doc: The convention-file reader (test seam).

members.name: fetch
members.doc: The `fetch` implementation used to post the PR comment (test seam).

members.name: remediate
members.doc:
Run the agent against the failure, optionally commit its changes, and ask
the executor to re-run the target as the verifier. Skips (no retry) on CI
unless {@link allowCI} is set, or if the agent run itself fails.

members.name: name
members.doc: A name for diagnostics — `"agent fix"`.

symbols.name: AgentResult
symbols.doc:
What an {@link AgentRunner} may resolve to: a {@link CommandOutput} (its
stdout is captured for the report), a string, or nothing.

symbols.name: AgentRunner
symbols.doc:
Runs the coding agent against the assembled {@link AgentContext}. The agent is
expected to edit files in place; the executor then re-runs the target. Throw
(or let the underlying command throw) to signal the agent could not run.

symbols.name: aiCache
symbols.doc:
Construct an {@link AiCache}, applying an optional configure lambda so it can
be set up inline — e.g. `aiCache((c) => c.dir(".cache").ttl(3600))`.

symbols.name: AiCache
symbols.doc:
A best-effort cache of AI provider responses, keyed by a {@link stableHash} of
each call's salient parts and persisted through a {@link CacheStore} (the
default writes JSON files under {@link AiCache.dir}). Configure it inline with
{@link aiCache}: set the {@link AiCache.dir}, the {@link AiCache.ttl}, or
{@link AiCache.disable} it entirely, then read with {@link AiCache.get_} and
write with {@link AiCache.put_}.

members.name: dir
members.doc: Directory for the default file store (default ".zuke/ai-cache").

members.name: ttl
members.doc: Entries older than this many seconds are ignored (default 604800 = 7 days; 0 = never expire).

members.name: disable
members.doc: Turn the cache off programmatically ({@link get_} misses, {@link put_} is a no-op).

members.name: store
members.doc: Inject a custom backing store (test seam; overrides the file store).

members.name: now
members.doc: Clock seam for `createdAt` and TTL checks (default `Date.now`).

members.name: enabled_
members.doc: INTERNAL: whether the cache is active.

members.name: key_
members.doc: INTERNAL: derive a stable key from the given parts.

members.name: get_
members.doc: INTERNAL: fetch a live (non-expired) entry, or `undefined`.

members.name: put_
members.signature: put_(key: string, text: string, usage?: Usage): Promise<void>
members.doc: INTERNAL: store a response under `key`.

symbols.name: aiFixer
symbols.doc:
Construct an {@link AiFixer} and apply the configuration lambda. Plug the
result into a target with `.recoverWith(...)`:

```ts
test = target()
  .executes(() => DenoTasks.test((s) => s.allowAll()))
  .recoverWith(aiFixer((f) => f.provider("claude").apiKey(this.key)));
```

symbols.name: AiFixer
symbols.doc:
A fluent AI fixer. Construct one via {@link aiFixer}, configure it, and attach
it to a target with `.recoverWith(...)`. Only `.provider(...)` and
`.apiKey(...)` are required; everything else defaults.

members.name: provider
members.doc: Set the model provider (required).

members.name: apiKey
members.doc: Set the API key, from a secret parameter or a literal string (required).

members.name: model
members.doc: Override the model (default: the provider's recommended model).

members.name: effort
members.doc: Set the thinking-effort hint (honoured by Claude; ignored elsewhere).

members.name: criteria
members.doc: Project-specific notes appended to the prompt (idioms, constraints).

members.name: conventions
members.doc:
Supply the project conventions text directly, instead of letting the fixer
read `CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md`. Pass an empty string to send none.

members.name: diff
members.doc: Configure the diff source used for context (default: the working tree).

members.name: include
members.doc: Only include diff sections matching these globs in the prompt context.

members.name: exclude
members.doc: Exclude diff sections matching these globs from the prompt context.

members.name: maxDiffTokens
members.doc: Cap the context diff at roughly this many tokens (default 16000).

members.name: autoApply
members.doc:
Apply the proposed fix to the working tree and ask the executor to re-run
the target. Off by default (the fixer only diagnoses). Writes are confined
by {@link allowPaths}, the built-in exclusions, and {@link maxEdits}, and
are refused on CI unless {@link allowCI} is set.

members.name: allowPaths
members.doc: Restrict applied edits to paths matching these globs (default: all).

members.name: excludePaths
members.doc: Exclude paths matching these globs from edits, on top of the built-ins.

members.name: maxEdits
members.doc: Cap how many files a single applied fix may touch (default 10).

members.name: allowCI
members.doc: Permit auto-apply (and committing) on CI; off by default.

members.name: commitFixes
members.doc:
After applying a fix, stage it, commit it, and push to the current branch —
so a healed pull request carries the fix as a commit. Implies
{@link autoApply}. Requires a checkout that can push (a non-detached branch
with credentials); a failed push is reported, not fatal.

members.name: commitMessage
members.doc: Override the commit subject used by {@link commitFixes}.

members.name: noPush
members.doc: Commit the fix but do not push it (leave it staged in a local commit).

members.name: comment
members.doc: Also post the diagnosis/fix as a PR comment (on by default).

members.name: noComment
members.doc: Do not post a PR comment (the job summary is still written).

members.name: suggest
members.doc:
On GitHub, post each code location as an inline review comment with a
committable `suggestion` block (the Copilot-style suggestion) instead
of a single overview comment. On by default; a no-op off GitHub or when the
model reports no specific locations, where the overview comment is used.

members.name: noSuggest
members.doc: Post a single overview comment instead of inline GitHub suggestions.

members.name: commentToken
members.doc: The token used to post the PR comment (defaults to the host's env var).

members.name: retry
members.doc: Retry the provider call on transient failures (see {@link RetryOptions}).

members.name: quiet
members.doc: Suppress the console printout (the summary/comment are still written).

members.name: fetch
members.doc: The `fetch` implementation for the API call (test seam).

members.name: exec
members.doc: The `git` runner used for the diff and commit (test seam).

members.name: write
members.doc: The file writer used when applying edits (test seam).

members.name: readFile
members.doc: The convention-file reader (test seam).

members.name: env
members.doc: The environment reader used to detect CI and the comment host (test seam).

members.name: budget
members.doc:
Attach a shared {@link Budget} that caps spend by an exact **token** count
(a USD cap is opt-in, computed from prices you supply to the budget). Once
the cap is reached the fixer skips the model call (and reports it) rather
than running up the bill; share one budget across reviewers and fixers to
bound a whole build.

members.name: cache
members.doc:
Reuse a prior fix for an identical failure (same provider, model, and
prompt) instead of calling the API again — see {@link AiCache}. Helpful when
the same failure recurs across CI re-runs; a hit does not draw down the
{@link budget}.

members.name: remediate
members.doc:
Diagnose the failure and, when permitted, apply (and commit) the fix. Always
reports; returns `{ retry: true }` only when it changed the working tree so
the executor re-runs the target as the verifier.

members.name: name
members.doc: A name for diagnostics — `"AI fix"`.

symbols.name: AiReviewError
symbols.doc: Raised when a reviewer is misconfigured, the API fails, or the gate trips.

members.name: name
members.doc: The error name.

symbols.name: aiReviewWorkflow
AiReviewWorkflowSpec
symbols.doc: What to generate — only `reviewers` is required.

members.name: reviewers
members.doc:
The reviewers whose key env vars and `.comment()` setting drive the
generated workflow. Each reviewer's `.apiKey(param)` parameter becomes an
`env:` entry (or its host equivalent) that maps the secret in; any
reviewer with `.comment()` causes the workflow to grant the right
commenting scope and pass the host's token env var.

members.name: host
members.doc:
The CI host the workflow targets. Defaults to `"github"`. Use `"gitlab"`,
`"azure"`, or `"bitbucket"` to generate the equivalent for those hosts.

members.name: target
members.doc: The build target the workflow runs. Defaults to `"review"`.

members.name: baseBranch
members.doc:
The base branch the diff is taken against (used by the GitHub workflow's
fetch step). Defaults to `"master"`.

members.name: path
members.doc: Output path. Defaults to the host's conventional location.

members.name: name
members.doc: Workflow name shown in the host's UI. Defaults to `"AI Review"`.

members.name: timeoutMinutes
members.doc: Per-job timeout in minutes. Defaults to 15.

symbols.name: Assessment
symbols.doc: The structured result of a review.

members.name: score
members.doc: Overall risk score, `0` (none) to `10` (severe).

members.name: severity
members.doc: The overall severity.

members.name: summary
members.doc: A one-line summary of the assessment.

members.name: findings
members.doc: The individual findings.

symbols.name: AssessmentFinding
symbols.doc: A single issue reported by the model.

members.name: title
members.doc: A short title for the issue.

members.name: severity
members.doc: The issue's severity.

members.name: id
members.doc:
A stable fingerprint for the finding, assigned by the reviewer (see
{@link "./suppress.ts".findingFingerprint}). Copy it into the suppress
list to dismiss a recurring false positive.

members.name: file
members.doc: The file the issue is in, if the model attributed one.

members.name: line
members.doc: The line the issue is at, if the model attributed one.

members.name: detail
members.doc: A longer explanation, if provided.

symbols.name: AssessmentType
symbols.doc: The kind of review an assessment performs.
symbols.signature: type AssessmentType = generic | security | secrets | correctness | license

symbols.name: budget
symbols.doc:
Construct a {@link Budget}, applying an optional configure lambda so caps can
be set inline — e.g. `budget((b) => b.maxTokens(100_000).maxCost(1))`.

symbols.name: Budget
symbols.doc:
A running token and cost budget for AI provider calls. Build one with
{@link budget}, set caps with the fluent setters, then fold each call's usage
in with {@link Budget.record_} and gate further calls on
{@link Budget.exhausted_}.

members.name: maxTokens
members.doc: Cap total tokens (input + output across all recorded calls).

members.name: maxCost
members.doc:
Cap estimated USD cost. Only takes effect for models you've priced via
{@link prices} — no prices ship by default — so the estimate uses your own
current rates. Pair it with {@link maxTokens} for a guaranteed hard cap.

members.name: prices
members.doc:
Supply per-model prices (USD per 1,000,000 tokens, keyed by model id) so a
{@link maxCost} cap and the cost estimate can be computed. Merges across
calls. Nothing is priced until you call this — provider pricing changes too
often to bake a table in, so the rates here are yours to keep current.

members.name: exhausted_
members.doc: INTERNAL: whether a configured cap has already been reached.

members.name: record_
members.signature: record_(usage: Usage | undefined, model: string): void
members.doc: INTERNAL: fold one provider call's usage into the running totals.

members.name: spend_
members.doc: INTERNAL: a snapshot of consumption so far.

members.name: remainingTokens_
members.doc: INTERNAL: remaining tokens before the cap, or undefined when no token cap.

members.name: describe_
members.doc: INTERNAL: a one-line human summary, e.g. "1,234 tokens (~$0.01) of 10,000 / $1.00".

symbols.name: BudgetSpend
symbols.doc: A snapshot of what a {@link Budget} has consumed so far.

members.name: calls
members.doc: How many provider calls were recorded.

members.name: inputTokens
members.doc: Total input (prompt) tokens across all recorded calls.

members.name: outputTokens
members.doc: Total output (completion) tokens across all recorded calls.

members.name: totalTokens
members.doc: Total tokens (input + output) across all recorded calls.

members.name: cost
members.doc: Estimated USD cost, when at least one recorded call had a known price.

symbols.name: CacheEntry
symbols.doc: A cached provider response.

members.name: text
members.doc: The model's raw text response.

members.name: usage
members.doc: Token usage reported for the original call, if any.

members.name: createdAt
members.doc: Epoch milliseconds when the entry was written (for TTL).

symbols.name: CacheStore
symbols.doc: A pluggable backing store for the cache (the default is file-backed).

members.name: get
members.doc: Fetch the entry stored under `key`, or `undefined` when absent.

members.name: set
members.doc: Store `entry` under `key`, replacing any prior value.

symbols.name: Confidence
symbols.doc: The model's confidence that a fix is correct.

symbols.name: correctnessReviewer
symbols.doc: A reviewer that scores the diff for correctness bugs and regressions.

symbols.name: DiffFetch
symbols.doc:
The base-branch fetch a {@link DiffSettings.fetchBase} requested — the remote
to fetch from and the branch (auto-detected from CI when unset).

members.name: branch
members.doc: The branch to fetch, or `undefined` to auto-detect it from the CI env.

members.name: remote
members.doc: The remote to fetch from (default `"origin"`).

symbols.name: DiffSettings
symbols.doc: Fluent diff source configuration passed to {@link "./reviewer.ts".Reviewer.diff}.

members.name: base
members.doc: Review the diff against `ref` (e.g. `"origin/main"`).

members.name: staged
members.doc: Review the staged changes (`git diff --cached`).

members.name: text
members.doc: Review a diff supplied directly, bypassing `git` (useful in tests).

members.name: fetchBase
members.doc:
Fetch the base branch (a shallow, tag-less `git fetch`) before diffing, and
diff against it — so CI needs no manual `git fetch` step. With no `branch`,
the base is auto-detected from the CI environment (GitHub's `GITHUB_BASE_REF`
— the pull request's base branch). Honoured by the {@link
"./fixer.ts".AiFixer}; if the fetch fails it falls back to the working-tree
diff.

members.name: text_
members.doc: The literal diff text supplied via {@link DiffSettings.text}, if any.

members.name: fetch_
members.doc:
The base-branch fetch requested via {@link DiffSettings.fetchBase}, or
`undefined` when none was requested.

members.name: argv_
members.doc: The `git` argv this diff source resolves to.

symbols.name: Effort
symbols.doc: The thinking-depth hint passed to providers that support it (Claude).
symbols.signature: type Effort = low | medium | high | xhigh | max

symbols.name: EnvReader
symbols.doc: Read an environment variable, tolerating an absent `--allow-env` permission.

symbols.name: FileEdit
symbols.doc: A whole-file edit: the complete new contents of one file.

members.name: path
members.doc: Repository-relative path of the file to write.

members.name: content
members.doc: The complete new contents of the file (not a patch).

symbols.name: findingFingerprint
symbols.doc:
A stable fingerprint for a finding: hash of the assessment kind, the
normalised title (trimmed, lowercased, whitespace collapsed), and the file.
Independent of line number so a finding keeps its id as code shifts.
symbols.signature: function findingFingerprint(assessment: AssessmentType, finding: AssessmentFinding): string

symbols.name: Fix
symbols.doc: The structured result of a fix attempt.

members.name: diagnosis
members.doc: A one-line explanation of what failed and why.

members.name: rootCause
members.doc: The underlying root cause the fix addresses.

members.name: confidence
members.doc: The model's confidence that the edits resolve the failure.

members.name: locations
members.doc: The specific code locations the fix targets, with verbatim source.

members.name: edits
members.doc: The whole-file edits that, applied together, should fix the failure.

symbols.name: FixLocation
symbols.doc:
A specific code location the fix targets: the exact offending source quoted
verbatim, its file and line(s), and the suggested replacement. Rendered as a
diff in the report so the comment shows real code, not just prose.

members.name: file
members.doc: Repository-relative path of the file.

members.name: line
members.doc: The 1-based line where the offending code starts.

members.name: endLine
members.doc: The 1-based line where it ends, when it spans more than one line.

members.name: code
members.doc: The exact offending source line(s), quoted verbatim.

members.name: suggestion
members.doc: The suggested replacement for {@link code} (empty means delete it).

symbols.name: GateRule
symbols.doc: A configured rule for {@link "./reviewer.ts".Reviewer.failWhen}.

symbols.name: GateSettings
symbols.doc: Fluent gate configuration passed to {@link "./reviewer.ts".Reviewer.failWhen}.

members.name: scoreAbove
members.doc: Fail when the assessed risk score is strictly above `value` (0–10).

members.name: severityAtLeast
members.doc: Fail when the overall severity is at least `value`.

members.name: rules_
members.doc: The configured gate rules, in the order they were added.

symbols.name: genericReviewer
symbols.doc:
A general-purpose reviewer scored on code quality and maintainability. Pair
with `.criteria(...)` to add project-specific notes (idioms, conventions, a
coding-style document); the built-in rubric is sufficient without them.

symbols.name: licenseReviewer
symbols.doc: A reviewer that scores the diff for license and compliance risk.

symbols.name: ModelPrice
symbols.doc: Per-model price in USD per 1,000,000 tokens.

members.name: input
members.doc: USD per 1,000,000 input (prompt) tokens.

members.name: output
members.doc: USD per 1,000,000 output (completion) tokens.

symbols.name: Provider
symbols.doc: A supported model provider.

symbols.name: RetryInfo
symbols.doc: What happened before a retry, for {@link RetryOptions.onRetry}.

members.name: attempt
members.doc: The attempt that just failed (1-based).

members.name: attempts
members.doc: The total number of attempts that will be made.

members.name: delayMs
members.doc: How long the helper will wait before the next attempt, in milliseconds.

members.name: reason
members.doc: Why the attempt failed — e.g. `"HTTP 503"` or `"timed out after 60000ms"`.

symbols.name: RetryOptions
symbols.doc: Configurable knobs for {@link retryingFetch}.

members.name: attempts
members.doc: Total attempts (first try + retries). Defaults to {@link DEFAULT_ATTEMPTS}.

members.name: baseDelayMs
members.doc: Backoff for the first retry; doubles each subsequent retry.

members.name: timeoutMs
members.doc: Per-attempt timeout in milliseconds (default 60s). `0` disables it.

members.name: onRetry
members.doc: Invoked before each retry, so a caller can report progress.

members.name: sleep
members.doc: Sleep seam — overridden in tests so retries don't take real time.

symbols.name: Reviewer
symbols.doc:
A fluent AI reviewer. Construct one via {@link securityReviewer} (and the
sibling factories), configure it, and attach it to a target with
`.validateBefore(...)` / `.validateAfter(...)`. `.provider(...)` and
`.apiKey(...)` are required; everything else has a default.

members.name: provider_
members.doc: The model provider, once `.provider(...)` has been called.

members.name: apiKey_
members.doc: The configured API key (a parameter — for its env var — or a literal).

members.name: commentEnabled_
members.doc: Whether `.comment()` is set — i.e. this reviewer posts to the PR.

members.name: commentToken_
members.doc: The configured comment-posting token, if `.commentToken(...)` was called.

members.name: provider
members.doc: Set the model provider (required).

members.name: apiKey
members.doc: Set the API key, from a secret parameter or a literal string (required).

members.name: model
members.doc: Override the model (default: the provider's recommended model).

members.name: effort
members.doc: Set the thinking-effort hint (honoured by Claude; ignored elsewhere).

members.name: criteria
members.doc:
Optional project-specific notes appended above the diff in the user prompt
— framing that fine-tunes the built-in rubric (e.g. "strict TypeScript,
no `any`/`as`"). Works for every reviewer; the assessment's own system
prompt already covers what to look for, so this is purely additive.

members.name: diff
members.doc: Configure the diff source (default: the working-tree diff, `git diff`).

members.name: include
members.doc: Only review files matching these globs (default: all files).

members.name: exclude
members.doc: Exclude files matching these globs (in addition to lockfiles).

members.name: maxDiffTokens
members.doc: Cap the diff at roughly this many tokens, truncating the rest.

members.name: failWhen
members.doc: Choose the gate that breaks the build (default: score above 7).

members.name: onError
members.doc:
What to do when the review itself fails (API error, refusal, bad JSON):
`"fail"` breaks the build (default), `"warn"` logs and passes.

members.name: retry
members.doc:
Retry the provider call on transient failures (`HTTP 408/429/500/502/503/
504` and network errors). The default is on — three attempts with
exponential backoff and `Retry-After` honoured. Pass an object to override:
`{ attempts: 5 }` to retry more, or `{ attempts: 1 }` to disable.

members.name: skipIfKeyMissing
members.doc:
Skip the review (instead of failing) when the API key is missing — handy
when the key is a CI-only secret. The skip is announced on the console and
in the job summary so the gap is visible.

members.name: comment
members.doc:
Also post the review to the pull/merge request as a comment. Works on
every supported CI host — GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Azure Pipelines,
Bitbucket Pipelines — dispatched at runtime by {@link detectCiHost}. A
single comment per reviewer is kept up to date across re-runs. A no-op
outside a PR context (e.g. local runs). On each host the workflow must
grant the right scope: GitHub `pull-requests: write`, GitLab a token with
the `api` scope, Azure `System.AccessToken`, Bitbucket an app password.

members.name: commentToken
members.doc:
The token used to post the PR/MR comment. Defaults to the active host's
conventional env var: `GITHUB_TOKEN` (GitHub), `GITLAB_TOKEN` (GitLab),
`SYSTEM_ACCESSTOKEN` (Azure), `BITBUCKET_TOKEN` (Bitbucket).

members.name: githubToken
members.doc: Backwards-compatible alias for {@link commentToken}.

members.name: quiet
members.doc: Suppress the findings printout and the job-summary section.

members.name: fetch
members.doc: The `fetch` implementation for the API call (test seam).

members.name: exec
members.doc: The `git` runner used to produce the diff (test seam).

members.name: env
members.doc:
Environment reader used to auto-detect the base branch for
{@link "./diff.ts".DiffSettings.fetchBase} (reads `GITHUB_BASE_REF`). A test
seam; defaults to the process environment.

members.name: budget
members.doc:
Attach a shared {@link Budget} that caps spend by an exact **token** count
(a USD cap is opt-in, computed from prices you supply to the budget). Pass
the same budget to several reviewers and a fixer to bound the whole build:
once the cap is reached, further reviews are skipped (not failed) with a
note, rather than running up the bill.

members.name: cache
members.doc:
Reuse a prior model response for an identical review (same provider, model,
and prompt) instead of calling the API again — see {@link AiCache}. A cache
hit costs nothing and does not draw down the {@link budget}.

members.name: suppress
members.doc:
Hide findings whose stable ID is in a {@link Suppressions} list — a learned
set of dismissed false positives. Every finding is fingerprinted and its ID
surfaced in the report, so dismissing one is a copy-paste into the list.

members.name: validate
members.doc:
Run the review and gate the build. Throws an {@link AiReviewError} when the
gate trips (or on a configuration/API error with `onError: "fail"`).

members.name: name
members.doc: A name for diagnostics — `"<assessment> review"`.

symbols.name: secretsReviewer
symbols.doc: A reviewer that scans the diff for leaked secrets and credentials.

symbols.name: securityReviewer
symbols.doc: A reviewer that scores the diff for security vulnerabilities.

symbols.name: Severity
symbols.doc: A severity level, ordered `none` < `low` < `medium` < `high` < `critical`.
symbols.signature: type Severity = none | low | medium | high | critical

symbols.name: suppressions
symbols.doc:
Construct a {@link Suppressions}, applying an optional configure lambda so the
file and inline fingerprints can be set inline — e.g.
`suppressions((s) => s.file(".zuke/suppress.json").add("abc"))`.

symbols.name: Suppressions
symbols.doc:
A file-backed set of suppressed finding fingerprints. The effective set is
the union of the fingerprints read from {@link Suppressions.file} and any
added inline with {@link Suppressions.add}; the reviewer drops a finding whose
{@link findingFingerprint} is in that set.

members.name: file
members.doc: Path of the JSON suppress list (default ".zuke/ai-suppress.json").

members.name: add
members.doc: Add fingerprints inline (in addition to any from the file).

members.name: reader
members.doc: Reader seam for the suppress file (default reads from disk, missing -> undefined).

members.name: load_
members.doc: INTERNAL: the effective set of suppressed fingerprints (file ∪ inline).

symbols.name: Usage
symbols.doc:
Token counts a provider reported for a review call, when the response carries
them. Each field is optional because not every provider reports every count.

members.name: inputTokens
members.doc: Tokens in the prompt / input.

members.name: outputTokens
members.doc: Tokens in the model's output / completion.

members.name: totalTokens
members.doc: Total tokens, reported by the provider or derived from input + output.

packages.name: @zuke/otel
packages.summary: `@zuke/otel` — an OpenTelemetry (OTLP/HTTP **JSON**) export plugin for Zuke

symbols.name: otel
OtelSettings
symbols.doc:
Configuration for the OTLP exporter, set through a lambda passed to
{@link "./plugin.ts".otel}. Every setter returns `this` so calls chain, and
each value falls back to the matching `OTEL_*` environment variable when left
unset (see {@link resolveOtel}).

members.name: endpoint
members.doc: Set the base OTLP/HTTP endpoint; `/v1/traces` and `/v1/metrics` are appended.

members.name: tracesEndpoint
members.doc: Set a full endpoint URL for the trace signal (no path is appended).

members.name: metricsEndpoint
members.doc: Set a full endpoint URL for the metric signal (no path is appended).

members.name: serviceName
members.doc: Set the `service.name` reported to the collector.

members.name: header
members.doc: Add one HTTP header to every export request.

members.name: headers
members.doc: Merge a map of HTTP headers into the export requests.

members.name: resourceAttribute
members.doc: Add one OTLP resource attribute.

members.name: resourceAttributes
members.doc: Merge a map of OTLP resource attributes.

members.name: timeout
members.doc: Set the per-request timeout (a duration string like `"5s"`).

members.name: endpoint_
members.doc: The base OTLP/HTTP endpoint (e.g. `http://localhost:4318`).

members.name: tracesEndpoint_
members.doc: A full endpoint for the trace signal, overriding {@link endpoint_} + `/v1/traces`.

members.name: metricsEndpoint_
members.doc: A full endpoint for the metric signal, overriding {@link endpoint_} + `/v1/metrics`.

members.name: serviceName_
members.doc: The `service.name` resource attribute.

members.name: headers_
members.doc: Extra HTTP headers sent on every export (e.g. an auth token).

members.name: resourceAttributes_
members.doc: Extra OTLP resource attributes (e.g. `deployment.environment`).

members.name: timeoutMs_
members.doc: Per-request timeout in milliseconds (default 10s).
